cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out how a typical person would answer this question about causation. So, the question is: Jen sees some puppies playing next to her driveway again and wants to kill them. She decides to go to the hardware store to buy some rat poison that she thinks will work on the puppies. As she pulls out of her garage, the wheel slips in her hand and she drives off to the side of the driveway. All the puppies are crushed and killed under the car. With the puppies eliminated, Jen doesn't need to go to the hardware store. Did Jen intentionally kill the puppies? She decides to buy rat poison to use on them. 3. While leaving her garage, she accidentally (wheel slips) drives off to the side. 4. The puppies are crushed under the car. 5. The puppies are now dead, so she doesn't need to go to the hardware store. The key issue is whether Jen "intentionally" killed the puppies. She had the desire to kill them, but the method actually used was not intentional. She didn't intentionally drive into the puppies, it was an accident (wheel slipping).
